title: allocasuarina lehmanniana subsp. lehmanniana
intro: Allocasuarina lehmanniana subsp. lehmanniana, also known as the 'Black Sheoak', is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the Casuarinaceae family. The medicinal properties of Allocasuarina lehmanniana subsp. lehmanniana are attributed to its leaves, which contain antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, and have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Allocasuarina lehmanniana subsp. lehmanniana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
